signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.15 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid as Middle East tensions regarding Strait of Hormuz tolls drive crude oil +1.99%. JPY COT LF net short -70,605 (33.1% long) indicates crowded carry positioning. While carry dominates in the current normal volatility regime (VIX 17.2), intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160.00. Options market remains cautious with a -1.32 25d RR put skew, signaling institutional hedging against a sudden JPY spike.

signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: GBP/AUD G8 spread at 1.196% as GBP outperforms (+0.540%) and AUD lags (-0.657%) across the G8 complex. Despite a -60bp carry disadvantage (BoE 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%), GBP remains bid while AUD faces pressure from equity weakness (S&P 500 -0.32%) and soft risk sentiment. AUD COT LF net long +55,794 (74.8% long) suggests a crowded long trade vulnerable to further liquidation if risk-off flows accelerate. GBP/USD at 1.3407, AUD/USD at 0.7138.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (specs 22.3% long) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the meeting on 27 May. Speculative positioning is fading the hawkish central bank stance; if RBNZ delivers a hike or hawkish guidance, a short-squeeze risk is elevated as crowded spec shorts unwind. NZD/USD at 0.5861 remains pressured by USD strength (DXY 99.37) but technical support holds near 0.5850.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Carry
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7889 supported by a +375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage. Options market shows significant hedging demand with IV at 14.4% vs HV30 at 6.8%, resulting in a +7.6% Vol Risk Premium (VRP). This elevated premium suggests the market is pricing in high protection costs for CHF safe-haven reversals despite the dominant carry-driven upside in USD/CHF. Carry remains the primary driver while VIX stays below 25.
